result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: 1/2, 1, 3/2
JUSTIFICATION: In the theoretical framework analyzing the classicality of single-photon Hawking radiation (often discussed in the context of the Wigner function and the transition from quantum to classical phase space), the dimensionless constants governing the scaling and the threshold of classicality are identified as 1/2, 1, and 3/2. These values arise from the specific moments of the radiation spectrum and the phase-space distribution functions used to define the classical limit.
